role_notes: A highly valuable theoretical analysis proving that looped transformers can simulate standard graph algorithms with size-independent parameters.
Introduces an equilibrium-based approach (DEQ) to neural algorithmic reasoning, moving away from step-by-step recurrence.
Proposes an open-book retrieval-augmented method for algorithmic reasoning, facilitating multi-task generalization.
Introduces PUZZLES, a high-quality evaluation benchmark focusing on logic and reinforcement learning within algorithmic reasoning.
A highly cited and foundational method for improving arithmetic reasoning in transformers using relative digit position embeddings (Abacus Embeddings).
Provides a rigorous, foundational analysis of transformer scaling regimes and representational capabilities when solving graph algorithms.
Proposes decoupling discrete and continuous data flows for perfect out-of-distribution algorithmic generalization.
Develops a primal-dual bipartite GNN framework targeting approximation algorithms for combinatorial problems.
Introduces Tropical Attention, a geometrically-grounded mechanism to enhance algorithmic reasoning for NP-hard problems.
